title: cyperus eragrostis f. melanocephalus
intro: Cyperus eragrostis f. melanocephalus, also known as the purple papyrus or black stem sedge, is a subspecies of the Cyperus eragrostis plant, which is a type of grass-like perennial that belongs to the sedge family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus eragrostis f. melanocephalus include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic and to treat respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional remedies in the form of poultices and salves.
